data.description:
## testing_mode: GREEN

Create .github/required-checks.txt as the canonical single source-of-truth listing all 11 required GitHub check-context names. This file is the coordination point for fc8b-aab0, d025-87d4, 792b-eb80, and 4383-9234.

## File content

Create the file at: .github/required-checks.txt

Format: one check-context name per line; lines starting with # are comments and are ignored.

Required entries (11 total, coordinated with upstream stories):
- From story d025-87d4 (CI workflow template): lint-format, tests, llm-review, validate-ci
- From story 792b-eb80 (platform matrix): Ticket tests (linux-bash4), Ticket tests (macos-bash3), Ticket tests (alpine-busybox)
  NOTE: GitHub check-context names for matrix jobs use the job 'name:' field: 'Ticket tests (linux-bash4)' etc. — verify against .github/workflows/ticket-platform-matrix.yml job name template.
- From story 4383-9234 (pipefail-strict): pipefail-strict (to be added when that story creates the job)
- Existing CI jobs (if included as required checks): ShellCheck, Hook Tests, Script Tests

IMPORTANT: Before writing, verify the exact context names by reading:
1. .github/workflows/ci.yml — job 'name:' fields
2. .github/workflows/ticket-platform-matrix.yml — job 'name:' template (matrix.name expansion pattern)
The required-checks.txt MUST use the exact strings GitHub will report as check-context names.

Add inline comments explaining which story owns each check and what the format rules are.

## Done Definition
.github/required-checks.txt exists, has ≥8 non-comment entries (placeholder for pipefail-strict acceptable), includes a header comment explaining format and ownership, and test_required_checks_file_exists in test-provision-ruleset.sh now PASSES.
data.title: GREEN: Create .github/required-checks.txt as single source-of-truth check-context names
